title: Characterization of Thermophilic Cellulases from Talaromyces leycettanus and Their Synergistic Action
author: Yuan Gu
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8g4htyw
materials: Purification of recombinant Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A
Fractions showing cellulase activities were pooled and further desalted with a 5 kDa molecular cut-off concentration tube (Millipore) using 0.1 M McIlvaine buffer (pH 3.5 or 4.5).
Residual activities were determined as described above.

steps.title: The full length cellulase-encoding genes, Tlcel5A...
steps.instruction: The full length cellulase-encoding genes, Tlcel5A and Tlcel6A , were identified in the genome sequence of T. leycettanus JCM12802, and obtained by PCR amplification with specific primers GH5F/GH5R and GH6F/GH6R (Table 1).

steps.title: The PCR conditions were as follows
steps.instruction: The PCR conditions were as follows: 95°C for 3min, followed by 30 cycles of 95°C for 30 s, 60°C for 60 s, and 72 ◦C for 1min.

steps.title: cDNAs were then synthesized in vitro...
steps.instruction: cDNAs were then synthesized in vitro using the ReverTra Ace-a-™ kit. The cDNAs coding for the mature Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A were amplified with primers GH5F1/GH5R1 and GH6F1/GH6R1 harboring restriction sites, respectively. (Table 1).

steps.title: The PCR products were inserted into...
steps.instruction: The PCR products were inserted into plasmid pGEM-T Easy and then transformed into E. coli Trans1-T1.

steps.title: The nucleotide and amino acid sequences...
steps.instruction: The nucleotide and amino acid sequences were used for BLAST analysis at NCBI (http:// www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/BLAST/).

steps.title: Simplified sequence alignment and prediction of...
steps.instruction: Simplified sequence alignment and prediction of the isoelectric point and molecular weight were conducted by using the Vector NTI Suite 10.0 software.

steps.title: Signal peptide prediction was carried out with SignalP 4
steps.instruction: Signal peptide prediction was carried out with SignalP 4.1 Server ( http://www.cbs.dtu.dk/services/SignalP/ ).

steps.title: Multiple alignment of protein sequences was...
steps.instruction: Multiple alignment of protein sequences was performed with the ClustalX 2.1 (http://www.clustal.org, [1], and the results were demonstrated by ESPript 3.0 (http://espript.ibcp.fr/ESPript/cgi-bin/ESPript.cgi, [2].

steps.title: The NetNglyc server was used to...
steps.instruction: The NetNglyc server was used to predict the putative N-glycosylation sites ( http://www.cbs.dtu.dk/services/NetNGlyc/ ).

steps.title: And the modeled structures were predicted...
steps.instruction: And the modeled structures were predicted and visualized by using the SWISS-MODEL [3] and Pymol [4].

steps.title: The cDNA fragments coding for the...
steps.instruction: The cDNA fragments coding for the mature proteins of Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A were digested with restriction enzymes Sna bI or Eco RI and Not I and subcloned into plasmid pPIC9 to yield expression vectors pPIC9- Tlcel5A and pPIC9- Tlcel6A , respectively.

steps.title: The recombinant plasmids were then linearized...
steps.instruction: The recombinant plasmids were then linearized with Bgl II and individually transformed into P. pastoris GS115 competent cells by electroporation.

steps.title: MD plates can be used to...
steps.instruction: MD plates can be used to select the transformants and pick some positive colonies into 3 mL BMGY with 10 mL tubes for two days and change 1 mL BMMY for another two days at 30 °C and 220 rpm.

steps.title: The culture supernatants were collected by...
steps.instruction: The culture supernatants were collected by centrifugation at 5,000 rpm for 10 min, followed by cellulase activity assay as described below.

steps.title: The most active transformants of Tl...
steps.instruction: The most active transformants of Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A were grown in 1 L shake-flasks containing 400 mL BMGY at 220 rpm and 30 °C for 2 days, respectively.

steps.title: The cultures were pelleted by centrifugation...
steps.instruction: The cultures were pelleted by centrifugation and resuspended in 200 mL of BMMY for 3-day-growth at 30 °C and 200 rpm. Methanol was then added at the final concentration of 0.5 % every 24 h for continuous induction of 3 days.

steps.title: The culture supernatants were collected by...
steps.instruction: The culture supernatants were collected by centrifugation at 4500× g for further analysis.

steps.title: The viva flow 200 ultrafiltration membrane...
steps.instruction: The viva flow 200 ultrafiltration membrane system (Sartorius, Germany) with 5 kDa cut-off was used for the concentration and buffer exchange (to 0.1 M Tris-HCl, pH 8.0) of the crude enzymes.

steps.title: The HiTrapTM Desalting column was used...
steps.instruction: The HiTrapTM Desalting column was used to desalt and the HiTrap Q Sepharose XL FPLC column (GE Healthcare) pre-equilibrated with 0.1 M Tris-HCl (pH 8.0) was used to purify for the recombinant proteins.

steps.title: The gradient NaCl of 0‒0
steps.instruction: The gradient NaCl of 0‒0.7 M at the flow rate of 3 mL/min was used to elute the proteins.

steps.title: The purified proteins were separated on...
steps.instruction: The purified proteins were separated on 12 % sodium dodecyl sulfate-polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis (SDS-PAGE) for purity and molecular mass analysis.

steps.title: Protein concentration was determined by using the Bradford method
steps.instruction: Protein concentration was determined by using the Bradford method.

steps.title: Deglycosylation of the purified recombinant Tl...
steps.instruction: Deglycosylation of the purified recombinant Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A To remove N-glycosylation, the purified recombinant Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A were treated with Endo H for 2 h at 37 °C following the manufacturer’s instructions (New England Biolabs), and checked by SDS-PAGE.

steps.title: Enzyme activity measurements The cellulase activities...
steps.instruction: Enzyme activity measurements The cellulase activities were determined using 1 % (w/v) CMC-Na, barley β-glucan or lichenan as the substrate.

steps.title: The reaction mixtures containing 100 μL...
steps.instruction: The reaction mixtures containing 100 μL of properly diluted enzyme solution and 900 μL substrate solution in 0.1 M McIlvaine buffer (pH 3.5 and 4.5) were incubated at 75 °C or 80 °C (optimum temperature) for 10 min. (When using 5 mg/mL Avicel as the substrate, the reaction period was lengthened to 60 min)

steps.title: The amounts of reducing sugars were...
steps.instruction: The amounts of reducing sugars were determined via the 3,5-dinitrosalicylic acid (DNS) method. [5] One unit (U) of enzyme activity was defined as the amount of enzyme producing 1 μmol of reducing sugar per minute under the assay conditions.

steps.title: Biochemical characterization of Tl Cel5A and...
steps.instruction: Biochemical characterization of Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A CMC-Na, barley-glucan and PASC were used as the substrates for enzyme characterization.

steps.title: The optimal pH was determined at...
steps.instruction: The optimal pH was determined at 75 °C for Tl Cel5A and 80 °C for Tl Cel6A, respectively, in 0.1 M McIlvaine buffer with pH ranging from 2.0 to 8.0.

steps.title: To test the pH stability, the...
steps.instruction: To test the pH stability, the purified enzymes were pre-incubated at 37 °C for 60 min in buffers with pH ranging from 1.0 to 12.0 (0.1 M KCl-HCl for pH 1.0‒2.0, 0.1 M McIlvaine buffer for 2.0 to 8.0, and 0.1 M glycine-NaOH for pH 9.0‒12.0).

steps.title: The residual enzyme activities were determined...
steps.instruction: The residual enzyme activities were determined at 75 °C and pH 3.5 for Tl Cel5A and 80 °C (barley β-glucan) or 70 °C (PASC) and pH 4.5 for Tl Cel6A, respectively.

steps.title: To determine the optimal temperature, the...
steps.instruction: To determine the optimal temperature, the cellulase activities were determined at temperatures ranging from 30 °C to 90 °C and pH 3.5 for Tl Cel5A or pH 4.5 for Tl Cel6A for 10 min, respectively.

steps.title: For thermal stability assays, Tl Cel5A...
steps.instruction: For thermal stability assays, Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A were incubated at temperatures of 70 °C, 75 °C or 80 °C and pH 3.5 or 4.5 for different periods of time.

steps.title: For half-life determination, 0
steps.instruction: For half-life determination, 0.05 mg of Tl Cel5A or 0.2 mg of Tl Cel6A was incubated at 60 °C, 65 °C or 70 °C for 0.5‒24 h

steps.title: Substrate specificity and kinetic parameters Substrate...
steps.instruction: Substrate specificity and kinetic parameters Substrate specificity of Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A were determined by using 1 % (w/v) CMC-Na, barley β-glucan, Avicel, lichenan, laminarin, xyloglucan, birchwood xylan, konjac flour, locus bean gum, glucomannan, or 0.2 mM of p NPGlu, p NPGal, p NPC as the substrate.

steps.title: The kinetic parameters (Km, Vmax and...
steps.instruction: The kinetic parameters (Km, Vmax and kcat/Km) of Tl Cel5A were determined by incubating the enzyme with 0.25 to 10 mg/mL CMC-Na at pH 3.5 and 75 °C for 5 min.

steps.title: For Tl Cel6A, the kinetic parameters...
steps.instruction: For Tl Cel6A, the kinetic parameters were determined at pH 4.5 and 80 °C for 5 min with 0.25‒10.0 mg/mL barley β-glucan as the substrate.

steps.title: The enzyme activities were determined by using the DNS method
steps.instruction: The enzyme activities were determined by using the DNS method. The kinetic constants were calculated using the Lineweaver-Burk plots by GraphPad Prism 6.0 (http://www.graphpad.com/scientific-software/prism/).

steps.title: Analysis of the cellooligosaccharides hydrolysis products...
steps.instruction: Analysis of the cellooligosaccharides hydrolysis products The hydrolysis products of cellooligosaccharides by Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A were detected by high-performance anion exchange chromatography (HPAEC, model 2500, Dionex, Sunnyvale, CA).

steps.title: Purified Tl Cel5A (0
steps.instruction: Purified Tl Cel5A (0.05 U) or Tl Cel6A (0.02 U) was added into 1 mL of cellooligosaccharide solution containing 200 μg of cellotetraose, cellopentaose, or cellohexaose and incubated at 60 °C for 0 min, 1 min, 5 min, 30 min, 1 h, 4 h or 5 h.

steps.title: After enzyme inactivation by boiling water...
steps.instruction: After enzyme inactivation by boiling water bath, the hydrolysates were diluted 100 times with ddH 2 O, and 100 )" target="_blank" rel="noopener">µ L of each sample was injected into the column of HPAEC.

steps.title: The oligosaccharides were eluted by 100 mM NaOH
steps.instruction: The oligosaccharides were eluted by 100 mM NaOH. And the standards of oligosaccharides consisted of glucose, cellobiose, cellotriose, cellotetraose, cellopentaose, and cellohexaose.

steps.title: The amount of each hydrolysate was...
steps.instruction: The amount of each hydrolysate was calculated based on the peak area. One enzyme unit (U) was defined as the amount of enzyme to hydrolyze 1 µmol of substrate per minute under the assay conditions. The catalytic efficiency values (kcat/Km) of Tl Cel6A against cellooligosaccharides were calculated according to Xia et al. (2016).

steps.title: Synergistic of Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A on cellulose hydrolysis
steps.instruction: Synergistic of Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A on cellulose hydrolysis

steps.title: When using Avicel as the substrate,...
steps.instruction: When using Avicel as the substrate, Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A were combined at the ratios of 0.4:3.6, 0.8:3.2, 1.2:2.8, 1.6:2.4, 2.0:2.0, 2.4:1.6, 2.8:1.2, 3.2:0.8, 3.6:0.4 in the total amount of 4.0 mM and incubated with 5 mg/mL of Avicel in 50 mM McIlvaine buffer, pH 4.0 at 60 °C for 24 h. The amounts of reducing sugars released were determined using the DNS method.

steps.title: When using SECS or Avicel as...
steps.instruction: When using SECS or Avicel as the substrate, 8 μM of Tl Cel5A or Tl Cel6A alone, or 4 μM Tl Cel5A and 4 μM Tl Cel6A was incubated with 5 mg/mL substrate at pH 4.0 and 60 °C for 12 or 18 h.

steps.title: With 2
steps.instruction: With 2.5 mg/mL PASC as the substrate, 2 μM of Tl Cel5A or Tl Cel6A alone or 1 μM Tl Cel5A and 1 μM Tl Cel6A was added.

steps.title: The amounts of reducing sugars released...
steps.instruction: The amounts of reducing sugars released were determined by using the DNS method.

steps.title: The amounts of glucose released were...
steps.instruction: The amounts of glucose released were analyzed by a one-factor ANOVA of SPSS19.0 to assess the synergistic effects of Tl Cel5A and Tl Cel6A. Statistical differences were considered to be significant at P < 0.05.
